title: Xtra Chewy Middle Eastern Lentil Tabbouleh
description: A hearty and flavorful Middle Eastern dish featuring brown lentils, nuka-pickle, and a blend of fresh herbs, perfect for a satisfying meal prep option. This recipe combines the chewiness of lentils with the tanginess of nuka-pickle and a hint of mustard.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, bring 2 cups of water to a boil and add the brown lentils, cooking until they are tender and chewy.
In a large bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chopped nuka-pickle, parsley, mint, and red onion to the bowl and stir to combine.
Once the lentils are cooked, drain and rinse them with cold water, then add them to the bowl and stir to combine.
Cook the whole wheat bulgur according to package instructions and let it cool.
Add the cooked bulgur to the bowl and stir to combine.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve the tabbouleh chilled, garnished with additional parsley and mint if desired.
